steps.title: Spot the ladies' backward reasoning
steps.content: The passage's key signal is the final sentence: the ladies observe a distinction exists, so they conclude the Countess must have serious vices. But the passage already established her actual flaws are minor. This reveals that disapproval comes before justification—the ladies have predetermined their judgment.

steps.title: Match this pattern to the answer
steps.content: Choice D captures this pattern: residents are determined to disapprove despite reasons that are largely groundless. The other choices either claim the ladies have softened their disapproval (A), introduce her background (B), or suggest her behavior justifies dislike (C). That matches choice D — D is correct.
